wk102_w2.notes: Pure-move seam verification; no production code edited, did not commit, did not git add (Agent 01 commits at sprint close). KEY SEAM NOTES: (a) the module pruner fn is update_messages but the HUD wrapper MUST stay named update -- engine.py:819 self.hud.update() runs EVERY FRAME; the dedicated delegation test patches hud_messages.update_messages and calls hud.update() to pin exactly that forward (a rename/drop of the wrapper would silently stop pruning or AttributeError every frame). (b) add_message is the widest-blast-radius HUD method (~56 refs/17 files incl pin_alert_watcher.py:24 getattr reach) -- the test pins the exact name+signature+COLOR_WHITE default (single-arg add_message('def') asserts color==COLOR_WHITE) so the 56 callers + the getattr probe keep resolving. (c) the 3 wrappers do a LAZY function-local 'from game.ui import hud_messages' then call the module attr, so the delegation spies monkeypatch the names ON hud_messages (patch-where-used). (d) messages are EVENT-DRIVEN and auto-expire after message_duration=3000ms, so the before/after captures are a weaker steady-state guard than for always-on chrome -- the load-bearing render proof is the W2 pixel-band assertion through the moved render_messages path + my own ad-hoc 2-message PNG (viewed: green/orange lines at top_bar_height+10). Documented the Z2 before-shot transient double-render of the Tip line as expected event-driven non-determinism (not a regression). Used a real headless HUD(1920,1080) under SDL_VIDEODRIVER=dummy for the behavior/render tests and object.__new__(HUD) bare instances for the wrapper spies. No regressions found; all 7 DoD criteria A-G hold.

(1) FULL __dict__ PARITY vs HEAD for all 8 types (goblin/wolf/skeleton/spider/bandit/bandit_lord/demon_overlord/skeleton_archer) — catches a missed/changed/extra attribute OR a wrong attribute surface. Enum-valued `state` is normalized to (qualname,name) so the duplicate EnemyState class created by exec-ing the source is not mistaken for drift. (2) EMBEDDED-LITERAL per-type stat coverage (hp/max_hp/attack_power/speed/xp_reward/gold_reward/color/size, plus boss name/is_boss, attacker set, and skeleton_archer ranged fields attack_range/min_range/attack_cooldown_max/is_ranged_attacker), captured verbatim from the HEAD subclass __init__ bodies and cross-checked against the live HEAD source so the literals cannot drift. (3) ATTRIBUTE-SURFACE invariants: wolf/skeleton/skeleton_archer have NO attackers; non-boss types have NO name/is_boss; bosses have both. (4) SHIM STRUCTURE: each of the 7 shims is an Enemy subclass and Shim(0,0) stats == Enemy(0,0,key); ENEMY_STATS covers the 7 keys and excludes skeleton_archer; register_attacker is on the Enemy base and is gated (records for goblin, no-op + no attr materialised for wolf). No production edits.

notes: Tests use a pure-logic + fakes pattern: no Ursina window, no real GLB asset loading. Heavy dependencies (Entity, _finalize_kenney_scatter_entity, _environment_*_lists, _building_occupied_tiles, _build_terrain_ground_mesh, _set_static_prop_fog_tint) are patched via unittest.mock.patch.object so only the in-scope code path runs.

test_path_entities_are_tracked drives build_3d_terrain end-to-end on a 10x10 world with one horizontal row of PATH tiles. The PATH branch at lines 552-563 of ursina_terrain_fog_collab.py constructs the entity but never calls self.track_visibility_gated_terrain(path_ent, tx, ty). One-line fix per Phase 1B in the master plan.

test_culling_reapplies_after_fog_change reproduces the four step sequence (initial fog sync -> camera cull to one chunk -> /revealmap -> stationary cull). Fails today because cull_terrain_chunks only processes the became_hidden / became_visible sets, which are both empty when the chunk set is unchanged. Phase 1 composition fix (ent._ks_fog_visible && ent._ks_chunk_visible) is the intended remedy.

test_visible_rect_reasonable_at_default_camera patches game.graphics.ursina_renderer.camera with a FakeCamera at cam_y=70 above map center, calls _get_visible_tile_rect on a stub self (the method only uses module-level globals). Returned rect (0,0,249,226) covers 240/256 chunks = 93.8%. Phase 2 frustum tightening must drop this to <50%.

test_static_entity_count_reduced is gated with pytest.mark.skip(reason=...) and the skip body raises AssertionError so it cannot silently pass once unmarked. Re-enable after Phase 3 _batch_static_terrain_for_chunks lands.

qa_smoke FAIL is expected per the master plan (Section 3, Tool 2): 'These tests will initially FAIL (they test for the fixed behavior). That is intentional.' Once Agent 03 lands Phase 1 + 2 in Wave 2, all 3 active tests should turn green and qa_smoke should return to PASS.

Consider extending test_visible_rect_reasonable_at_default_camera with two more parametrized cases (cam_y=40 zoomed-in, cam_y=100 zoomed-out) once Phase 2 lands, to guard against regressions across the typical zoom band.

what_i_changed.summary: Appended TestFogOverlayPerf class with one test (test_ensure_fog_overlay_early_out_post_heightmap) plus two minimal fakes (_FakeFogTexture, _FakeTerrainGroundEntity). Test constructs the post-WK53 steady-state shape (heightmap active, _terrain_ground_entity set, _fog_entity=None, matched _fog_revision_seen) and asserts that across two ensure_fog_overlay calls with the same fog_revision, pygame.image.frombuffer (the loop body's terminal call before GPU upload) is invoked AT MOST ONCE. Under the buggy pre-fix gate, both calls run the rebuild body -> 2 invocations -> FAIL. Under Fix 1.A, both calls early-out -> 0 invocations -> PASS.

evidence.pre_fix_test_result: SIMULATED FAIL (confirmed). Methodology: ran an in-memory harness that monkey-patched UrsinaTerrainFogCollab.ensure_fog_overlay to mirror the committed-HEAD pre-fix gate (`if fog_revision == my_rev and _fog_entity is not None: return`). With the test's heightmap-active scenario (_fog_entity=None, matched _fog_revision_seen), the old gate fell through and the rebuild body executed on BOTH consecutive matched-revision calls -> pygame.image.frombuffer was invoked 2x -> primary assertion `frombuffer_calls <= 1` would have failed. Direct pre-fix run against committed game/ code was not performed because the task constraint forbids modifying or reverting game/ code in this lane; the in-memory simulation produces the same logical outcome and is reproducible from the test file plus a tiny harness snippet. The discriminator was further verified via boolean algebra: OLD gate `(rev_match) AND (_fog_entity is not None)` evaluates `True AND False = False` -> no early-out; FIX gate `(rev_match) AND (_fog_entity is not None OR _terrain_ground_entity is not None)` evaluates `True AND (False OR True) = True` -> early-out.
evidence.post_fix_test_result: PASS. Working-tree version of game/graphics/ursina_terrain_fog_collab.py:213-229 already contains Agent 03's Fix 1.A (the heightmap-aware gate). Test passes deterministically: frombuffer_calls=0 across two matched-revision calls (both early-out immediately at the gate). Single-test run + full-file run both green.

Active tests now: TestTerrainEntityTracking::test_path_entities_are_tracked, TestTerrainEntityTracking::test_culling_reapplies_after_fog_change, TestTerrainEntityTracking::test_visible_rect_reasonable_at_default_camera, TestTerrainChunkBatching::test_static_entity_count_reduced, TestFogOverlayPerf::test_ensure_fog_overlay_early_out_post_heightmap.

notes: Test uses unittest.mock.patch.object to spy on tfc.pygame.image.frombuffer and tfc.TerrainTextureBridge.refresh_surface_texture without touching the production code. This keeps the test purely observational.

Pre-fix demonstration was performed via an in-process monkey-patch harness rather than reverting game/ code, to honour the file-lane constraint. The simulation is bit-for-bit equivalent to the OLD gate logic and confirms the test would have failed against the committed (pre-Wave-6) code. Full reproducer recipe is preserved in commands_run[2] for future audit.

If a future change to ensure_fog_overlay re-introduces the bug (e.g., reverts the heightmap-aware clause), this test will FAIL deterministically, calling out the regression by name in its assertion message.

item_03_guardhouse_2_arrows.detail: GUARDHOUSE_ARROWS_PER_SHOT = 2 in config. defensive.py fires loop range(GUARDHOUSE_ARROWS_PER_SHOT) applying damage per arrow and emits per-arrow projectile events with slight offset. Backward compat kept via _last_ranged_event = first event.

item_05_building_destruction_rubble.detail: RubbleRecord dataclass with record_id, position, footprint, type, timestamps. cleanup_manager creates rubble on building destruction and appends to sim.rubble_records. sim_engine initializes rubble_records list, includes it in snapshot, and expires records after 2 minutes. snapshot.py has rubble_records: tuple = () field.

item_06_enemy_building_priority.detail: ENEMY_BUILDING_PRIORITY_RANGE_TILES=10 in config. enemy.py find_target() computes near_town flag by checking if any targetable building is within range. When near_town=True, building bias multipliers are lowered (castle 0.3, neutral 0.3, other 0.4) making buildings preferred targets. When far away, original biases preserved.

item_07_reversed_names_fixed.detail: Counter-flip logic iterates child attrs (_ks_name_label, _ks_gold_label, _ks_rest_label, _ks_hp_bg, _ks_hp_fg for heroes; _ks_hp_bg, _ks_hp_fg for enemies) and sets child.scale_x = abs(child.scale_x) * facing. This ensures child text/bars always render correctly regardless of parent facing direction.

item_08_building_name_labels_removed.detail: _sync_building_worldspace_ui() disables label_ent (label_ent.enabled = False) with comment 'WK61-FEAT-001: disabled'. Building names no longer rendered.

item_09_taxable_gold_g_key_toggle.detail: Gold display section reads held_keys.get('g', False). Gold text only shown when stash > 0 AND g_held is True. Otherwise gold_ent.enabled = False. WK61-FEAT-003 tag.

item_10_rubble_rendering.detail: _rubble_entities dict initialized in __init__. _sync_snapshot_rubble() called during sync. Method removes expired rubble, creates 3 scattered rock models per rubble record with deterministic RNG, uses sim_px_to_world_xz, terrain height, and dusty gray-brown color.

item_11_taxable_gold_marketplace_blacksm.detail: _render_taxable_gold() method shows 'Taxable Gold: $X' when stored_tax_gold > 0. Called after rendering marketplace, blacksmith, inn, and trading_post panels. Same pattern as guild_panel.

item_12_chat_button_hero_menu.detail: Chat button state tracked via _chat_button_rect and _current_hero. handle_click() returns {type: 'start_conversation', hero: hero} on click. Button rendered at bottom of hero info panel with hover highlight. WK61-FEAT-005 tags.

item_13_enemy_click_info_panel.detail: EnemyPanel class renders enemy type, HP bar, combat stats, state, and current target. engine.py has selected_enemy property, try_select_enemy() method with distance hit test, and includes selected_enemy in game state dict. input_handler.py cascades enemy selection after peasant selection attempt. sim_engine.py initializes selected_enemy = None.

bonus_enemy_sound_system.detail: EnemySoundManager with per-type sound map (goblin, wolf, skeleton, spider, bandit, plus shared sounds for skeleton_archer and bandit_lord). Distance-based volume attenuation, ambient cooldowns per enemy, simultaneous sound cap of 4. Three event hooks: on_enemy_attack, on_enemy_death, update_ambient. Clean design, fails silently on missing files.

evidence.issues_found: NONE. All 13 items and the bonus enemy sound system are correctly implemented.
notes: All 3 gate checks pass cleanly. No regressions detected.
Code review of all 13 items shows correct implementation with proper feature tags (WK61-TUNE-*, WK61-FEAT-*, WK61-BUG-*).
validate_assets warns=46 are all pre-existing missing_model_file placeholders — no new asset issues from R1.

The enemy building priority logic in enemy.py uses a bias multiplier approach rather than hard override, which preserves emergent behavior while still making enemies prefer buildings when near town. Good design.
The guardhouse multi-arrow implementation correctly applies damage per arrow AND emits separate projectile events for visual rendering. Backward compat maintained via _last_ranged_event alias.
Rubble lifecycle is clean: created on destruction, stored in sim_engine, serialized in snapshot, rendered in 3D, expired after 2 minutes.
Counter-flip fix uses abs(child.scale_x) * facing which correctly handles both directions without accumulating sign errors over multiple frames.

test_game_engine_update_advances_sim_tim.bug: Double advance: _prepare_sim_and_camera() advances _sim_now_ms by 50ms, then SimEngine.update() advances it again by 50ms. Actual delta=100ms for dt=0.05, expected 50ms.
test_game_engine_update_advances_sim_tim.root_cause: game/engine.py line 1228 and game/sim_engine.py line 605 both increment _sim_now_ms
test_game_engine_update_advances_sim_tim.fix_target: Wave 1 Task A (single sim time owner)

test_paused_does_not_advance_sim_time.bug: Pause leak: _prepare_sim_and_camera() advances _sim_now_ms BEFORE checking self.paused at line 1234. Paused ticks leak 50ms of sim time.
test_paused_does_not_advance_sim_time.root_cause: game/engine.py lines 1228-1229 run unconditionally before line 1234 pause check
test_paused_does_not_advance_sim_time.fix_target: Wave 1 Task A (single sim time owner)

test_destroying_building_creates_one_rub.note: SimEngine._cleanup_destroyed_buildings() runs first inside sim.update() and removes the building from the list. CleanupManager (engine-side) then finds no destroyed buildings, so rubble count is exactly 1. Guards the single-rubble invariant against ordering regressions.

test_destroying_building_emits_one_event.note: Same ordering: sim-side cleanup emits 1 BUILDING_DESTROYED event; CleanupManager finds nothing. Guards the single-event invariant.

architecture_findings.double_sim_time: CONFIRMED. Both GameEngine._prepare_sim_and_camera() and SimEngine.update() advance _sim_now_ms. In deterministic mode, each engine.update(dt) increments by 2*int(round(dt*1000)).
architecture_findings.pause_leak: CONFIRMED. _prepare_sim_and_camera() advances sim time before checking pause, then returns False. SimEngine.update() does not run, so only one of the two advances leaks — but it should be zero.
architecture_findings.redundant_cleanup: GameEngine._cleanup_destroyed_buildings() at engine.py:1157 is effectively dead code because SimEngine._cleanup_destroyed_buildings() at sim_engine.py:749 already removes all destroyed buildings. CleanupManager never finds anything to clean. The redundant call is safe (idempotent) but wastes cycles and creates maintenance risk.
architecture_findings.cleanup_ordering_fragility: If cleanup ordering changes (e.g., engine-side cleanup runs before sim-side), double rubble and double events would occur. The new tests guard against this.

starvation_scaling_30_heroes.reproducible: Yes -- 34 on both runs (same seed, deterministic).
starvation_scaling_30_heroes.interpretation: The keep-path/defer fix is correctly applied to ALL 7 caller sites (verified by grep: hero.py:949, enemy.py:304/538/575, guard.py:173, tax_collector.py:148/203 all guard with 'if _new_path is not None'), and the cap was bumped 12->24. It is effective at 10-hero scale (stuck_events=2) but does NOT hold at 30 heroes (34 ~= broken-WK63's 37). The keep-path guard only helps a unit that ALREADY HAS a path; under 30-hero contention many heroes still cannot acquire their FIRST path within the per-frame budget, so they sit with an empty path (nothing for follow_path to follow), do not advance toward goal, and trip stuck-recovery (max_stuck_ms~=4983 ~ 5s, near the stuck threshold). The 12->24 cap bump was insufficient to clear 30-hero first-path contention.

screenshot_verdict.scene_integrity: HEALTHY -- normal base-overview: CASTLE centered, all guild buildings render and are labeled (RANGERS, WARRIORS, SMITH, MARKET, INN, ROGUES, WIZARDS), HOUSEs, cross-shaped road network, grass/trees, fog edges. No corruption or artifacts.
screenshot_verdict.units: Heroes are spread across DISTINCT map locations (Nova bottom-left near GUARDS, Gareth right near MARKET, heroes between the two HOUSEs, at WARRIORS guild, near WIZARDS) -- not clustered at a single spawn, consistent with navigation working. Enemies present on the north road. Several units show green health bars mid-field. HUD: Gold 2100, Heroes 4, Enemies 43, Wave 1; perf overlay 'PF/s: 71 calls 16 fails 6.3ms'.
screenshot_verdict.verdict: PASS (visual) but NON-DECISIVE for the failing gate. The base_overview scenario uses only ~4 heroes (HUD), NOT the 30-hero stress condition. The static frame looks correct and shows units distributed (not frozen at spawn), and does not contradict the defect; but a single static image cannot confirm smooth motion under the 30-hero contention that fails, and a 4-hero scene would not exhibit it. The authoritative motion-quality signal is the headless 30-hero stuck_events=34.

blockers: BLOCKER for Phase A baseline commit (Step A4): 30-hero/1.0x stuck_events=34 fails the plan's acceptance (<= 5). Agent 04 must revisit the defer fix so it scales. Do NOT commit the Phase A baseline until this is <= 5. All other gates (full suite 506, determinism, qa_smoke incl 0.25x) are green.
recommended_next_actions: RETURN TO AGENT 04 (re-open Step A2): The keep-path guard alone does not fix 30-hero starvation because it only preserves an EXISTING path; heroes awaiting their FIRST path under contention still starve with an empty path. Candidate directions for Agent 04 (their call -- QA does not edit production code): (a) give heroes with NO path a deterministic same-frame fallback (e.g. move_towards/direct-steer toward goal) when the budget defers, so a path-less hero still advances instead of standing still; (b) prioritize first-path requests over re-plans within the budget; or (c) raise/retune the per-frame budget specifically for first-path acquisition. The 12->24 cap bump is necessary but not sufficient.

After Agent 04 iterates, Agent 11 re-runs ONLY the 30-hero check (python tools/observe_sync.py --seconds 48.0 --heroes 30 --seed 3 --log-every 9999 --qa --bounty) plus a full-suite + qa_smoke re-green; if stuck_events <= 5 and all gates pass, Phase A is GO for the Step A4 baseline commit.
The single test reconciliation in tests/test_wk63_engine_boundary.py is correct and can stay regardless of Agent 04's next iteration (it pins the corrected None-on-defer contract).

notes: ROOT CAUSE the prior tests skipped (and were latently broken): the default GameEngine(headless=True) sets sim.ai_controller=None (game/sim_engine.py:134; it is only wired externally via game/engine.py:619 setter / ursina_app.py:181 factory). The prior tests bound ai = engine.sim.ai_controller (None) and skipped earlier on 'if not heroes', so they never called ai.handle_moving -> the None controller was never exercised. Fix: ai = engine.sim.ai_controller or BasicAI(). BasicAI() takes no required args, sets hunger_behavior=hunger in __init__, and BasicAI.handle_moving delegates to bounty_pursuit.handle_moving (ai/basic_ai.py:364-365).

buy_meal: locate the default-world food_stand via ai.behaviors.hunger.find_nearest_food_stand(probe_hero, engine.sim.buildings); construct Hero(stand.center_x, stand.center_y, hero_class=warrior, hero_id=h_test_buy_meal); append to engine.sim.heroes; gold=999; state=MOVING; target={type:buy_meal,food_stand:stand}; target_position=(hero.x,hero.y); drive ai.handle_moving. snapshot_api.why_engine_not_sim: Plan skeleton suggested engine.sim.build_snapshot() but the SIM-level method REQUIRES per-frame kwargs (vfx_projectiles, screen_w/h, camera_x/y, zoom, default_zoom, paused, running, pause_menu_visible). The zero-arg renderer-facing contract is engine.build_snapshot() (confirmed by tests/test_renderer_snapshot_contract.py:76,94). LATER WAVES: use engine.build_snapshot() unless you supply all sim-level kwargs.
snapshot_api.copy_semantics: Entity LISTS are shallow-copied to tuples (membership frozen); individual entities are shared live references -> a mutation guard on entity fields is meaningful.

entity_fields_digested.method: defensive getattr with '<missing>' sentinel; floats rounded(6); enums/refs coerced via repr() for value (not identity) comparison

pin_1_render_dto_field_parity.pins: Move 3. Reads EXACTLY the fields the renderers read off live entities, via the same getattr-with-default, for first 3 heroes, 3 enemies, 5 buildings, 3 bounties. inside_building flattened to a center tuple (never a live ref). has_target is a bool (never the live target). entity_id keyed via hero_id||entity_id||id(). This is the tuple shape Agent 03 DTO builders + Agent 10 DTO consumers must reproduce.

pin_2_anim_one_shot.pins: Move 1a. At HeroRenderer + RendererRegistry level: a one-shot trigger (attack/hurt, loop=False clips) plays once -> finishes -> returns to base (idle); does NOT replay without a new trigger; a new identical trigger replays it. Documents current behavior (renderer consumes/clears the entity trigger via setattr) so the seq-based replacement preserves play-once-per-trigger.

pin_3_fog_discovery_digest.pins: Move 1b byte-identical guardrail. DETERMINISTIC_SIM=1, seed 3, no AI controller, a ranger walks two scripted legs across the map for 600 ticks. Grid-coverage digest (VISIBLE/SEEN tile counts + is_discovered building count) is the byte-stable fog STATE.

pin_4_snapshot_no_mutation_on_consume.pins: Extends WK65 build-time guard to CONSUME. Builds snapshot, drives a full RendererRegistry pass (animate + render every hero/enemy/peasant/guard/tax/building/bounty) against a dummy Surface, asserts the gameplay-field digest unchanged. suite_crash_fix.title: Full single-process suite crash (Windows fatal exception 0xC0000005) fixed without weakening any pin
suite_crash_fix.symptom: `python -m pytest -q` (INCLUDING tests/test_wk66_render_boundary.py) died mid-run with 'Windows fatal exception: access violation (0xC0000005)'; the same suite with --ignore=tests/test_wk66_render_boundary.py ran clean (575 passed, 4 skipped). pytest-forked / pytest-xdist not installed, so no per-test process isolation available.
suite_crash_fix.reproduced: Yes. First `python -m pytest -q` crashed; faulthandler traceback: game/graphics/font_cache.py:84 render_text_shadowed_cached -> building_renderer.py:109 _draw_center_label -> building_renderer.py:168 render -> registry.py:103 render_building -> test_wk66_render_boundary.py:582 _drive_full_render_pass -> :614 test_snapshot_not_mutated_on_consume. Crash localized to the consume/no-mutation render-pass pin.
suite_crash_fix.root_cause: The renderer pass these pins drive populates several MODULE-GLOBAL / class-level pygame.Surface (and pygame.font.Font) caches that persist for the whole process: font_cache._FONT_CACHE/_TEXT_CACHE/_TEXT_SHADOW_CACHE; HeroSpriteLibrary._cache / EnemySpriteLibrary._cache / WorkerSpriteLibrary._cache / BuildingSpriteLibrary._cache (AnimationClip frame surfaces, some built via convert_alpha() i.e. display-format-bound); ui_icons._type_cache/_tier_cache/_badge_cache. Those Surfaces are allocated under one pygame display session; this file's per-test `pygame.quit()` (the pinned test style) then tears down SDL, leaving the cached Surfaces/Fonts ORPHANED in the global dicts. A later test in the same process re-inits pygame, gets a cache HIT, and blits / pygame.transform.* the stale surface, corrupting the SDL/GDI heap until the process dies. The crash surfaces inside font.render() but the cause is the stale cached surfaces being reused across a pygame teardown, not the font call. The 575 other tests never drive a full render pass through these caches (e.g. test_wk65_snapshot_no_mutation only BUILDS the snapshot, never consumes it), so they stayed under Windows' surface limit; these pins were the straw. NOT dominated by the other 575 tests -> a file-local fix is correct (no conftest/escalation needed).
suite_crash_fix.hygiene_fix_applied: Added to tests/test_wk66_render_boundary.py ONLY: (1) a `_clear_global_render_caches()` helper that defensively .clear()s every one of the above module-global Surface/Font caches (each in its own try/except so a not-yet-imported module can't break teardown); (2) an autouse `_render_resource_hygiene` fixture that, AFTER each test in this module, calls `_clear_global_render_caches()` then `pygame.display.quit()` (if display init) and `pygame.quit()`. This guarantees the file leaves ZERO stale cached pygame surfaces/fonts behind, so a surface allocated by one of these pins can never be reused by a later suite test after a pygame teardown -> the access-violation cause is removed. No assertion, scenario, golden, or field list was changed.
suite_crash_fix.assertions_unchanged_confirmation: All 4 pins keep full rigor: pin 4 (no-mutation) STILL drives a real full RendererRegistry render pass over the snapshot (cache clearing only happens in teardown, AFTER the assertion); pin 1 (DTO parity) STILL checks the full UnitDTO/BuildingDTO/BountyDTO field lists; pin 3 (fog digest) is byte-identical to golden {visible:486, seen:313, discovered_buildings:1} under DETERMINISTIC_SIM=1; pin 2 (anim one-shot) unchanged. Only additive teardown was introduced.
suite_crash_fix.conftest_note: tests/conftest.py was NOT modified. The accumulation is caused solely by THIS file's render-pass + pygame.quit() pattern, so a file-local autouse fixture is the correct, minimal fix; no shared/conftest teardown and no pytest-forked install was needed.

wave2_anim_repin.title: Re-pinned the 2 anim one-shot pins to Agent 10's seq-based mechanism (Wave 2 / Move 1a) -- re-pin of the SAME behavior, WITH TEETH; also VERIFIES the new mechanism is correct
wave2_anim_repin.context: Agent 10 Wave 2 (Move 1a) landed: renderers no longer clear the trigger via setattr(entity, '_render_anim_trigger', None). The sim now bumps a monotonic entity._anim_trigger_seq on each NEW trigger (also exposed as UnitDTO.anim_trigger_seq), and each renderer plays the one-shot only when the seq advances vs a renderer-owned last-seen (pygame HeroRenderer.self._last_trigger_seq; Ursina self._unit_anim_state[obj_id]['last_seq']). The trigger STRING is no longer cleared off the entity, so it now LINGERS. The Wave-0 pins over-fit the OLD write-back mechanism and went red as the plan anticipated.
old_pins_asserted: test_anim_one_shot_plays_once_then_replays_on_new_trigger: after consuming a trigger, asserted attack_state._render_anim_trigger is None (the setattr-clear write-back -- gone by design); and that re-SETTING an IDENTICAL trigger string (without any seq concept) replays the one-shot.
test_anim_one_shot_via_registry_level: asserted getattr(hero, '_render_anim_trigger', None) is None after one registry pass (the write-back clear off the live entity -- gone).
wave2_anim_repin.new_seq_based_contract: Each renderer plays a one-shot ONLY when the sim's monotonic _anim_trigger_seq advances past the renderer's own last-seen seq. The trigger string is NOT cleared off the entity (it lingers). Driven via the REAL sim path Hero._queue_render_animation(name) (same call on_attack_landed makes), which sets _render_anim_trigger AND increments _anim_trigger_seq (verified: 0 -> 1 -> 2). render_state is the hero itself, so the registry reads the seq directly off the live entity.

teeth_against_three_failure_modes.never_play: Each pin asserts that an ADVANCED seq starts the one-shot clip (renderer current/clip == 'attack'). If a seq advance failed to play, this fails.
teeth_against_three_failure_modes.loop_forever: _advance_until_base() raises if the one-shot does not release within a 400-frame budget; pins then assert the clip is back on the base (idle). Ursina pin rewinds the record's t0 past the clip length (the elapsed input _frame_index_for_clip reads) to deterministically drive the finished-branch, then asserts clip=='idle', oneshot is False.
teeth_against_three_failure_modes.replay_on_unchanged_seq: THE KEYSTONE TEETH for Move 1a. After the one-shot finishes, the trigger STRING still lingers on the entity (asserted) but the seq is UNCHANGED -> pins tick the renderer 60x (pygame) / call _compute_anim_frame 30x (Ursina) and assert NO replay: pygame asserts _anim_lock_one_shot stays None every frame; Ursina asserts the record's t0 is NOT reset and last_seq stays pinned. This is the exact hazard introduced by removing the write-back clear (the string now lingers).

ursina_headless_coverage.how: UrsinaRenderer.__init__ builds a real Ursina window/Entity graph, so the instance is created via UrsinaRenderer.__new__(UrsinaRenderer) and ONLY the two attributes _compute_anim_frame reads are populated (_unit_anim_state={} + _clips_cache={}). The method itself, the clip metadata (HeroSpriteLibrary.clips_for -- pure pygame, no window) and _frame_index_for_clip are the REAL production code -- nothing is faked. The unchanged-seq/lingering-trigger case is the most dangerous on this path because GameEngine._update_render_animations (engine.py:1219-1238) copies _render_anim_trigger -> _ursina_anim_trigger EVERY tick the trigger is set but does NOT touch _anim_trigger_seq, so _compute_anim_frame sees the string every frame while the seq is static. The only value not naturally controllable headlessly is time.perf_counter(); the finished-branch is driven deterministically by rewinding the record's t0 (the elapsed input the method reads) rather than a flaky real sleep.
ursina_headless_coverage.follow_up_recommended: An Ursina combat-capture (live attack/hurt one-shot frame on a 3D billboard) is still the only thing not exercisable headlessly -- recommend folding it into the G2/G3 Ursina gate diff if/when Agent 03/10 register a combat capture scenario (currently the only registered Ursina scenario is wk61_hold_g_tax_overlay; no scripted melee one-shot scenario exists).

verification_that_teeth_are_real_mutatio: pygame replay-on-unchanged-seq: monkey-patched HeroRenderer.update_animation in-memory (NO production edit) to gate on the trigger STRING and ignore the seq (the old behavior). The 60-frame teeth loop CAUGHT it (_anim_lock_one_shot went non-None) -> teeth confirmed real.

Ursina replay-on-unchanged-seq: built a buggy _compute that restarts on every lingering-trigger tick (ignores last_seq); the t0-unchanged assertion CAUGHT it (t0 reset detected) -> teeth confirmed real.
loop-forever: _advance_until_base's frame budget raises AssertionError with a 'LOOP-FOREVER regression' message if the clip never releases.
wave2_anim_repin.real_bug_found_in_agent10_mechanism: NONE. Agent 10's seq mechanism is VERIFIED CORRECT on BOTH render paths. pygame HeroRenderer: new seq plays once (returns to idle after 117 frames at 1/60 dt), unchanged-seq + lingering trigger does NOT replay, seq bump replays. Ursina _compute_anim_frame: new seq -> clip='attack' oneshot=True last_seq=1; 30 unchanged-seq calls keep t0 fixed + last_seq=1 (no restart); after t0 elapses -> base 'idle' oneshot=False; seq bump -> 'attack' last_seq=2. No bending of any test to force a pass.

blockers_and_preexisting_flakiness.title: AI-decision digest drifts across in-process GameEngine builds (pre-existing global-state carry-over)
blockers_and_preexisting_flakiness.detail: With seeded heroes the digest is NOT reproducible across repeated in-process builds: 3 root causes found, same family as WK66 _fog_revision and WK65 test_spawner global-rebind. (1) ai/basic_ai.py _AI_RNG = get_rng('ai_basic') is module-level, shared by every BasicAI, never re-seeded per build (drives patrol/wander via ai._ai_rng.uniform). (2) game/entities/buildings/base.py RESEARCH_UNLOCKS module-level dict mutated by unlock_research() and never reset between tests -- changes blacksmith catalogue -> hero shopping/decisions. (3) tests/perf_ursina_stress.py:24 + tests/perf_stress_test.py:22 set os.environ['SIM_SEED']='42' at IMPORT time; pytest collection mutates the parent-process env, and config.SIM_SEED (read at import, re-applied by SimEngine) then overrides in-test set_sim_seed. This third one is what broke the keystone in the FULL suite while it passed in isolation.
blockers_and_preexisting_flakiness.mitigation_in_test: Keystone + reproducibility pins compute the digest in a FRESH SUBPROCESS whose env pins SIM_SEED=3 + DETERMINISTIC_SIM=1; _build_digest_engine additionally re-seeds set_sim_seed(3), basic_ai._AI_RNG, controller._ai_rng, and resets RESEARCH_UNLOCKS to all-False. All mitigation lives in the test; NO production code touched.
blockers_and_preexisting_flakiness.pm_followups: (a) re-seed _AI_RNG per BasicAI build; (b) per-engine research state or a research-reset fixture; (c) move SIM_SEED env writes in perf_*stress*.py into fixtures/tests, not module top. All latent test-isolation gaps, independent of the WK67 boundary moves.

notes: Root cause of the 5 failures was purely the test harness, not a behavior regression: do_shopping calls as_ai_view(view) then reads view.commands. A plain dict gets wrapped in ai.behaviors.view_compat._DictGameStateView, which exposes no .commands -> AttributeError. The fix passes a non-dict view (as_ai_view passes non-dicts through) carrying a REAL SimCommandSink.

Approach (b) (minimal sim stub + real SimCommandSink) was chosen over (a) (full headless GameEngine) for all 5 because it keeps each test's existing hero/economy/Marketplace objects and therefore its exact recorded assertions, while still exercising the genuine production write path (apply_hero_command -> hero.buy_item + economy.hero_purchase). No production code imported beyond game.sim.hero_commands.SimCommandSink.

The WK61 tax tests (#4,#5) confirm the BUILDING tax deposit, which happens inside hero.buy_item (resolved via the hero's pending shop), so routing through apply_hero_command preserves marketplace.stored_tax_gold exactly -- those assertions are untouched.

pin_5a_anim_to_tick.wave_item: 5a (Agent 10) — game/graphics/ursina_units_anim.py::anim_clock_seconds(frame_tick_id); under config.DETERMINISTIC_SIM returns int(frame_tick_id)*(1/20), else time.perf_counter(); both unit renderers read it.
pin_5a_anim_to_tick.what_it_pins: Forces config.DETERMINISTIC_SIM=True via monkeypatch and asserts the anim clock is a PURE FUNCTION of the sim tick: (a) same tick -> same value across calls; (b) strictly monotonic increasing with tick (0..200); (c) equals tick*(1/20) exactly; (d) the per-(tick,clip,frame) selection (the actual renderer-facing choice, via _frame_index_for_clip) is IDENTICAL across two independent passes over a representative clip set. Sanity guards that the non-looping attack one-shot actually reaches its finished pose and the looping walk advances (clock not stuck on 0).
pin_5a_anim_to_tick.headless_safe: yes — plain pygame.Surface frames need no display; no Ursina import.
pin_5a_anim_to_tick.agent10_manual_proof_referenced: per-(tick,clip,idx) digest cbecc2e6356b4475a1d293ad42825d6e7b74f69c0726798b85245cb87808cbd6 identical across two runs (cited in the pin's section comment).

pin_5b_fog_revision.wave_item: 5b (Agent 03) — root cause: class-global Peasant._spawn_counter carried over between builds, shifting a peasant idle offset -> a fog revealer's grid tile -> the _fog_revision increment COUNT (the +/-1 drift). Fix: reset Peasant._spawn_counter=0 in SimEngine.__init__ next to the seed reset.
pin_5b_fog_revision.what_it_pins: Computes the per-tick _fog_revision sequence (engine.sim._fog_revision) over a scripted ranger two-leg reveal (600 ticks, no AI controller so movement is fully scripted; mirrors tests/test_wk66_render_boundary.py::_run_fog_scenario) in TWO FRESH SUBPROCESSES with pinned env (DETERMINISTIC_SIM=1, SIM_SEED=3) and asserts the two sequences are BYTE-IDENTICAL. Sanity: parsed tuple length == 600 and final value > 1 (non-trivial evolving sequence).
pin_5b_fog_revision.why_subprocess: Same load-bearing reason as the keystone digest: pytest collection imports perf_*stress* modules that set os.environ['SIM_SEED']='42' at import; config.SIM_SEED is read at import and SimEngine re-applies it, OVERRIDING in-test set_sim_seed(). Pinning SIM_SEED in a fresh process makes the sequence byte-stable regardless of suite ordering.
pin_5b_fog_revision.observed: Both subprocess runs: 600-tick sequence, final _fog_revision = 61 (scripted scout reveal). Pre-pin spot check confirmed two same-seed IN-PROCESS builds also now agree (Agent 03 fix working); subprocess is the bulletproof unit. NOTE: the prompt's 'final=54 at 2000 ticks' is the full-game-loop figure; this pin uses a 600-tick scripted-scout scenario (final=61) chosen for a rich-but-fast deterministic reveal footprint without spawning/AI nondeterminism.

pin_5c_combat_capture.wave_item: 5c (Agent 12) — tools/screenshot_scenarios.py::ursina_melee_combat + tools/wk67_combat_capture_patch.py; `python tools/run_ursina_capture_once.py --scenario ursina_melee_combat ...` byte-identical across two DETERMINISTIC_SIM runs (Agent 12 manual proof SHA256 2DE5D5F4...).
pin_5c_combat_capture.what_it_pins: Does NOT run the Ursina capture (needs a display). Asserts the scenario stays REGISTERED + importable so the coverage can't silently disappear: (1) 'ursina_melee_combat' in URSINA_CAPTURE_SCENARIOS (the registry run_ursina_capture_once.py consumes) with the expected patch_path/default_ticks>0/stem via get_ursina_capture_scenario; (2) the pygame-path builder scenario_ursina_melee_combat is importable + callable (dispatched by get_scenario). pin_5c_combat_capture.headless_safe: yes — pure registry/import assertions; no display, no Ursina init.

findings.detail: Agent 08 G2 + H1 VERIFIED. Hire button: full real-input path deducts 100 gold, spawns 1 hero (warrior for warrior_guild, cleric for temple) into engine.heroes with home_building set, bumps guild.heroes_hired, emits hero_hired. Disabled paths (at-cap and gold<100) and non-hirable buildings (marketplace) all render hire_hero_button_rect is None so no hit-rect exists -> no hire possible. H1: a paused (non-modal) LMB inside the panel still opens the interior; the same paused LMB out in the world is dropped (selection untouched) whereas RUNNING it deselects. No case where hire or paused behavior fails -- Agent 08 does NOT need to go back.
notes: DEV_MODE (env KINGDOM_DEV_MODE=1) would set HERO_HIRE_COST=0 / infinite gold / cap 999; not set in test env so default cost 100 / cap 8 / gold 2100 hold.
No Temple exists in a fresh engine; the temple test appends a constructed game.entities.buildings.temples.Temple. Marketplace/WarriorGuild DO exist at game start (used directly).

hero_hired is queued by event_bus.emit and dispatched on flush(); the success test subscribes then flushes to capture exactly one event. Disabled-path tests probe handle_click directly (no live rect) to confirm no hire_hero dispatch + no state change.

world_snapshot_pin.seed: set_sim_seed(3); SIM_SEED env pinned to 1 in a fresh subprocess
world_snapshot_pin.why_subprocess_with_pinned_env: generate_heightmap's Perlin octaves use base=int(config.SIM_SEED); config.SIM_SEED is read from the env at import (config.py: int(os.getenv('SIM_SEED','1'))) and is NOT changed by set_sim_seed. Verified: SIM_SEED=42 changes the heightmap hash (aaa7d98f...) while the tile grid is unchanged (tiles use the world_gen sub-RNG seeded by set_sim_seed). Some suite modules (tests/perf_*stress*.py) set os.environ['SIM_SEED']='42' at import time which pytest collection inherits, so an in-process pin would FLAP by collection order - the snapshot is therefore computed in a fresh process with SIM_SEED=1 pinned, the SAME bulletproofing the WK67 keystone uses. The test also asserts the subprocess actually saw config.SIM_SEED==1.
world_snapshot_pin.noise_package: present in this env (heightmap not None); heightmap pin skips gracefully if the 'noise' package is ever unavailable (tile-grid pin still asserted).
